title: Validating HTML Tags with Strict Tag Names

statement.markdown: Write a function that validates HTML tags with strict tag names. A valid tag name is a string that does not contain any uppercase letters, starts with a lowercase letter, and does not contain any consecutive repeated letters. For example, 'p' is valid, but 'P' is invalid. 'aa' is invalid, and 'a' is valid. You will be given a list of strings representing HTML tags. Return `true` if a tag is valid, and `false` otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A list of strings representing HTML tags.
statement.outputFormat: A list of boolean values representing whether each tag is valid.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input will only contain letters, and that the input list will not be empty.

constraints.description: The number of tags in the input list.

examples.output: [true, false, true, false, false, true, true, false, false, false]
examples.explanation: The function should return `true` for tags that do not contain uppercase letters, start with a lowercase letter, and do not contain consecutive repeated letters.

hints.text: Check the length of the tag to ensure it is not empty.
Check if the tag starts with a lowercase letter.
Check if the tag contains any uppercase letters.
Check if the tag contains any consecutive repeated letters.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: The function should iterate over each tag in the input list, and for each tag, check the length, first character, and content for validity.
editorial.bruteForce: A simple brute force approach is to check each tag individually.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient solution would be to use a single pass over the tags.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution would be to use a single pass and a single data structure to store the state of the previous character.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The function should return `true` for valid tags and `false` for invalid tags.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check for uppercase letters.
Not checking for consecutive repeated letters.
